_comment: task-desk composes the task-tracker atoms. All date/dependency math is offline in tools/tasks.py, shipment normalization in tools/shipments.py, coverage in tools/coverage_verify.py. Every task cites a real source (anti-phantom); register writes gate on task_tracking; nothing is sent, invoiced, or posted without human confirmation. Tracking and verification only: no legal advice (contract-desk), no money send (finance-desk), no deal-stage moves (deal-pipeline).
description: Project task and obligation tracking: cited event-triggered tasks, backwards planning, waiting-on accountability, shipments, payment milestones, and deliverable coverage verification.

steps.input_from: obligation rows / user statements / shipment events
steps.condition: creating cited tasks; register write requires task_tracking

steps.input_from: a connected or pasted brand message
steps.condition: the source is an email; citation is code-stamped, body is untrusted

steps.input_from: the project's tasks + resolved event dates (or a deadline)
a task id + target state or hand-off event + source
steps.condition: action is a governed status change or waiting-on hand-off
action is a read-only outstanding-tasks view (waiting-on vs I-owe, due bands)

steps.input_from: a tracking number (live) or manual shipment fields
steps.condition: action is a shipment update; delivered_at feeds task-plan as the anchor

steps.input_from: the payment schedule + a deliverable event
steps.condition: action is billable readiness; ready milestones hand off to finance-desk / invoice-generate

steps.input_from: the required points + one or more media transcripts
steps.condition: action is deliverable coverage verification; abstains rather than infers

steps.input_from: the assembled output of steps 1 to 8
steps.condition: always; enforces the boundary, citations, no fabrication, and formatting before handoff

note: A single request may fire several steps (a shipment update recomputes the plan; an approval fires both a status change and billable readiness). deal-resourcing seeds tasks; contract-desk supplies obligation rows; finance-desk receives billable milestones. Every write is human-gated and every task cites a source.
